text.title: Chunking Strategies — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `chunking-strategies` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Chunking choice is driven by retrieval-recall eval, not by intuition.
rule.rationale: Without an eval-driven choice, you optimise the wrong knob.

rule.statement: Use structure-aware chunking (markdown heading split, AST-based code chunks) when document type has structure.
rule.rationale: Structure-aware chunking preserves semantic units; fixed-size splits mid-paragraph and loses retrieval.

rule.statement: Chunk overlap is bounded (0-20%). Higher overlap multiplies storage without recall gain.
rule.rationale: Overlap > 20% inflates vector store with diminishing returns.

rule.statement: Multi-type corpora use a dispatcher choosing strategy per document type.
rule.rationale: One-size-fits-all chunking on heterogeneous corpora produces uniformly mediocre retrieval.

rule.statement: Chunk size matches the embedding model's optimal input length (~256-512 tokens for most).
rule.rationale: Mismatched chunk size wastes tokens or degrades recall depending on direction.
